summary: Matched Peach's neutral special implementation by replacing the remaining ftPe_Init assembly slice with a new C source for ftPe_SpecialN/Toad behavior, wiring the new object into obj_files.mk, and adding the needed fighter/Peach struct names and prototypes. The diff also improved adjacent naming for fighter motion-state parameters, SpecialN facing direction, Peach fighter variables, and shield-related fields. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s, removing 889 lines of assembly that included Peach SpecialN and related helper labels.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialN.c with 424 lines implementing Peach's ground and aerial neutral special, neutral-special hit states, collision transitions, physics, animation, hitlag callbacks, item cleanup, and Toad item spawning.
Added public declarations in ftPe_SpecialN.h for ftPe_SpecialN_DoDeath2, ftPe_SpecialN_OnDeath2, and ftPe_SpecialN_IsActive, in addition to the existing SpecialN action callbacks.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialN.c.o instead of relying on the old assembly object.
Renamed Peach fighter variables at offsets fp+2240 and fp+2244 from x14/x18 to specialairn_used and toad_gobj, and added mv.pe.specialn.facing_dir at fp+2340.
Expanded ftPe_DatAttrs around +94 through +AC with named fields used by the SpecialN implementation, including specialairn_vel_x_div, specialairn_vel_y, x98, xA0, xA4, xA8, and xAC.
Changed Peach init/reset code to initialize specialairn_used to false and toad_gobj to NULL.
Retargeted Peach item cleanup callbacks in SpecialHi and SpecialLw from ftPe_Init_8011B704 to ftPe_Init_OnDeath2.
Updated it_27CF callsites/prototypes so item-side Peach Toad callbacks refer to ftPe_SpecialN_DoDeath2 and ftPe_SpecialN_IsActive.

Improved Fighter_ChangeMotionState signature and internal variable names: new_motion_state_index became FtMotionId msid, arg2 became MotionFlags flags, otherObj became arg3, and animation arguments became anim_start, anim_speed, and anim_blend.
Renamed fighter fields x19AC to specialn_facing_dir and x19B4/x19B8 shield-related fields to shield_unk0/shield_unk1, with linked updates in CLink, Link, Mars, and Peach code.
Added a glossary entry for div, presumably to support the new specialairn_vel_x_div naming.
smart_moves: Used small local enums for cmd_vars indices and states, replacing raw command-variable slots with cmd_phys_state, cmd_anim_state, cmd_accessory4, cmd_hit_anim_state and explicit state enum names.
Factored shared ground/aerial behavior into helpers such as reset, doAnim, setupColl, setupHitColl, doHitAnim, and common coll_mf flags instead of duplicating every SpecialN and SpecialAirN path.
Kept matching-only stack padding guarded behind #ifdef MUST_MATCH with explicit TODO comments, preserving decomp matchability without making the normal code path rely on fake locals.
Preserved item lifecycle semantics by setting held-item spec to toad_gobj, installing OnDeath/OnTakeDamage callbacks when the item exists, and clearing those callbacks in ftPe_SpecialN_DoDeath2.
Modeled hitlag behavior through onEnterHitlag/onExitHitlag callbacks that forward to it_802BDFA0 and it_802BDFC0 only when toad_gobj is non-NULL.
Centralized ground-air transition flags in a static MotionFlags const coll_mf, making the exact state-preservation mask reusable across normal and hit collision transitions.
Improved API types in Fighter_ChangeMotionState to FtMotionId and MotionFlags, reducing ambiguity for future action-state matching work.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter special moves, naming cmd_vars slots with local enums makes event-script-driven state changes much easier to audit than raw fp->cmd_vars[N] accesses.

FighterVars offsets should be named as soon as behavior is understood; here fp+2240 became Peach's specialairn_used latch and fp+2244 became toad_gobj, clarifying both aerial physics and item cleanup.
Ground/aerial paired actions can often share animation, collision, and hit-state helpers while still selecting the correct return callback, such as ft_8008A2BC for ground and ft_800CC730 for air.

Item-backed special moves need cleanup and hitlag callbacks to survive state transitions; setup functions should reinstall callbacks after Fighter_ChangeMotionState when the spawned item still exists.
MotionFlags constants are safer and more searchable than inline numeric masks when preserving animation, hit status, item visibility, model flags, and command update behavior across state changes.

Struct/header cleanup can be part of a matching PR when the C implementation exposes enough evidence for better names, but broad renames should be tied to concrete callsites as done for Fighter_ChangeMotionState and shield_unk fields.
If a match requires otherwise-unused stack storage, isolate it under MUST_MATCH and document it instead of obscuring the real logic.
naming_conventions: Peach neutral-special functions use the ftPe_SpecialN and ftPe_SpecialAirN prefixes for normal states and ftPe_SpecialNHit / ftPe_SpecialAirNHit for hit states.
Static callbacks in ftPe_SpecialN.c are named by role: onAccessory4, onHitAccessory4, onEnterHitlag, onExitHitlag, onUnkHit.
Internal shared helpers use do*/setup* names for action fragments: doAnim, doColl, doAirColl, doHitAnim, setupColl, setupHitColl.
Command-variable enum names use cmd_* for indices and *_state_N for value enums, for example cmd_phys_state and phys_state_2.
Peach-specific fighter vars are stored under fp->fv.pe.* and motion vars under fp->mv.pe.specialn.*.
The matched item pointer at fp+2244 is named toad_gobj, while the aerial-use latch at fp+2240 is named specialairn_used.
Fighter_ChangeMotionState parameters are typed and named semantically: FtMotionId msid, MotionFlags flags, anim_start, anim_speed, anim_blend.
Field x19AC is named specialn_facing_dir, and x19B4/x19B8 are named shield_unk0/shield_unk1.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a full assembly file with a C implementation and updated obj_files.mk so the C object participates in the build.
Used address comments on static prototypes, such as /* 11E174 */ and /* 11EC6C */, to map C helpers back to the original assembly labels.
Used MUST_MATCH-only dummy stack arrays in several functions where the original assembly required unused stack space.
Preserved exact animation/collision transition semantics with Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls carrying current animation frame, speed 1, blend 0, and a shared coll_mf bitmask.
Matched boolean/null checks around toad_gobj before forwarding to item functions, mirroring assembly guard branches.
Kept small wrapper IASA functions empty where the original assembly was just blr.
Introduced typed MotionFlags and FtMotionId usage to avoid raw integer drift while still matching existing motion-state logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
One in-code TODO says an unused stack remains for matching under MUST_MATCH.
One in-code TODO says doAirHitColl can maybe be combined with doAirColl; this is author-side code commentary, not reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Peach Toad item functions it_802BDE18, it_802BE214, it_802BDF40, it_802BDFA0, it_802BDFC0, or it_802BE100.
Find PRs that further refine ftPe_DatAttrs fields x98, xA0, xA4, xA8, and xAC used by Peach SpecialN.
Find PRs that remove or reduce MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays in ftPe_SpecialN.c.
Find PRs that combine doAirHitColl with doAirColl or otherwise refactor Peach SpecialN collision helpers.
Find PRs that improve shield_unk0 and shield_unk1 names after this provisional rename.
